quotes: Perfect praise at the beginning, in the middle, and at the end belongs solely to Allah. Acknowledging His blessings with the heart, the tongue, and one's actions is the key that unlocks the doors of mercy for all of creation.
The peak of the soul's liberation is when we submit ourselves entirely and only to Allah, and the pinnacle of strength is when we entrust all our affairs and seek help solely from Him.
A servant's need for Allah's guidance (the straight path) in every single breath is far more urgent than his body's need for food and drink.
reference: Surah Al-Fatihah: 6 (Majmu' Al-Fatawa Ibn Taymiyyah / Tafsir Ibn Kathir)

quotes: The Quran is a perfect guide without the slightest doubt, but its guiding light will only penetrate and benefit the hearts of those who are righteous and mindful of Allah (Al-Muttaqun).
Make patience and prayer your guiding light and helper. Prayer performed with true humility (khushu') will feel light and become the soul's strongest fortress when the storms of trials strike.
Remember Allah in your times of ease, and He will remember and help you in your times of hardship. Dhikr (remembrance of Allah) is the lifeblood of the heart and the key to true tranquility.

The phrase of Istirja' (Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji'un) is not merely a verbal utterance during grief, but a profound pledge that we and everything we love belong purely to Allah and will inevitably return to Him.

Allah is ever near. He always hears and answers the prayers of His servants who call upon Him. Therefore, perfect your good thoughts (husn al-zhann) of Him and never tire of knocking on the door of His mercy.

It may be that you dislike something, while it is actually profoundly good for you. Entrust your life's choices to the One Who perfectly knows the future, for human intellect is severely limited in seeing the ultimate end of destiny.
No matter how heavy the trials and burdens of today may be, rest assured that the Creator has already measured your shoulders to bear them. Allah does not burden a soul beyond its capacity.

Human hearts lie between the fingers of Ar-Rahman (The Most Merciful). Praying for steadfastness upon His guidance is the supplication of those with deep knowledge, recognizing how weak faith is without His protection.
True unity of the Muslim Ummah can only be achieved by holding fast to the rope of Allah (the Quran and Sunnah). Division is a pit of destruction that must always be avoided.
The sign of true piety is not the absence of anger, but the ability to suppress it and having the expansiveness of heart to forgive others' mistakes even when we have the power to retaliate.
Grief and feelings of weakness are human nature, but faith will lift us back up. Do not be consumed by worldly, fleeting sorrows, for the highest honor always resides in the faith within your chest.
The life of this world is nothing but a deceiving enjoyment. True victory is not amassing as much of the world as possible, but being saved from the Hellfire and admitted into Paradise.

Tawhid (Islamic monotheism) is the foundation of all deeds, while dutifulness to parents is the highest manifestation of noble character. Worshipping Allah alone and being good to others are two inseparable rights in Islam.
Trusts (Amanah) are deposits that will be accounted for in the Hereafter. Fulfilling trusts to their rightful owners and judging fairly are the keys to establishing goodness on earth.

The only scale of truth when disputes arise is to return to the Quran and the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Obedience to creation has its limits and is nullified if it involves disobedience to the Creator.

Every blessing and good thing we receive is purely out of Allah's grace, while disasters and bad things happen as a consequence of the sins from our own hands. This is the most crucial foundation for constant self-reflection.
The path of truth was forged by the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and his Companions. Deviating from the path of the early generations of believers is the path of destruction and the worst of destinations.

Help one another in acts of righteousness and piety, but do not help one another in sin and transgression. True friendship is one that strengthens each other upon obedience to Allah, not one that justifies falsehood.

This religion of Islam is already perfect. Adding new religious innovations (bid'ah) that were not exemplified by the Messenger of Allah ﷺ is not an improvement, but a form of audacity and a veiled rejection of the perfection of Allah's favor.
True justice is tested when we deal with someone we hate. Hatred must never shift us from the scale of objectivity, because being just is closer to piety.

The generation beloved by Allah is those who are humble towards their fellow believers, yet possess honor and firmness ('izzah) against the enemies of the religion, and are unafraid of the blame of critics in defending the truth.

A servant's primary responsibility is to guard their own piety. The misguidance of others will not harm you as long as you remain upon guidance and have fulfilled the obligation of enjoining good and forbidding evil.

This world is like a brief resting place that often deceives with its amusements. People of intellect are those who are not distracted, but actively gather provisions of Tawhid and righteous deeds for the eternal home of the Hereafter.

Not a single leaf falls in the darkness of the night except under Allah's watch. Absolute certainty in Allah's infinite knowledge is the ultimate cure for all anxieties of the heart when facing unexpected destinies.

Security of the soul in this world and absolute salvation in the Hereafter are promised only to those who purify their Tawhid, without mixing their faith with even a speck of shirk (the greatest injustice).

The path of truth (Sunnah) is only one and straight, whereas the paths of misguidance are many and adorned with doubts. Hold fast to the one straight path so that this Ummah is not scattered into ruin.

The pinnacle of sincerity is when a servant dedicates all of their prayers, worship, life principles, down to the moment of their death purely to the Lord of the worlds, without associating any partners with Him.

Making mistakes is human nature, but true nobility lies in how quickly we imitate the example of Prophet Adam: immediately acknowledging our wrongs and begging for forgiveness, rather than seeking justification for our sins.

Wearing your best clothes when heading to the mosque is a manifestation of glorifying the symbols of Allah. Islam teaches moderation; enjoying halal provisions and adorning oneself without falling into the valley of arrogance or extravagance.

Pray with a soft voice and a heart full of submission. Indeed, Allah is All-Hearing of the most silent cries of His servant, and He does not love those who transgress the limits in their supplications.
The key to the blessings of provision, security, and prosperity in life is not merely found in relentless worldly effort, but in piety and faith that are deeply rooted in the Lord of the universe.

Be forgiving, enjoin humanity to do good, and turn away from the ignorant. These three things are the foundation of noble character that will guard your heart's tranquility from the wicked tongues of people.

The sign of a living heart is when it trembles in submission upon hearing the name of Allah, and its faith increases when pondering over His verses. The truthfulness of this faith is then proven by perfect reliance (Tawakkul) upon Him.
Know that the true life for the soul and intellect is only attained by responding to the call of Allah and His Messenger ﷺ. A heart that turns away from His Sharia is, in reality, a dead heart.
The sweetest fruit of piety (taqwa) is being granted 'Furqan'—a light of knowledge within the heart capable of sharply distinguishing between truth and falsehood amidst the striking storms of doubt.
Disputes and divisions within the ranks of Muslims are the gateways to weakness and the loss of dignity. Obedience, unity upon the true methodology (manhaj), and patience are the keys to victory.
Allah will never revoke the blessings of security, provision, and glory from a nation, unless that nation itself destroys them through disobedience and abandoning submission to Him.
Sadness is a trial of the heart, but it will vanish with the certainty that Allah is always with His servant who relies upon Him. Allah's help often arrives right at the peak of hardship.
No matter how great a servant's sins are, the doors of His forgiveness are always wide open. Allah not only accepts sincere repentance but also purifies the servant's soul with His boundless mercy.
Honesty is a companion that will never betray its owner. Fear Allah and always stand in the ranks of those who are truthful in their tongues and hearts, so you may be saved from hypocrisy.
The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was an immensely compassionate envoy. The suffering of his Ummah weighed heavily upon him, and he deeply desired salvation and faith to adorn all of our hearts.
When people turn away and disappoint you, Allah is sufficient as the best support. It is upon Him that we rely, for He is the Lord of the Mighty Throne.
The Quran is a cure for diseases of the heart such as doubts, desires, and misconceptions. A heart consistently touched by its verses will find guidance, mercy, and true peace.
The gift of Islamic guidance and the sweetness of faith are far more worthy of being celebrated in the heart than all the wealth and worldly luxuries gathered by mankind.
Not a single second of our actions, words, or heartbeats escapes Allah's surveillance. The awareness of this (Muraqabah) is the firmest foundation of piety.

The close allies (Awliya) of Allah are not those who possess supernatural powers, but rather those who combine sound faith with steadfast piety. For them, there is no fear in the Hereafter and no grief over this world.
No calamity can be lifted, and no blessing can be attained, except by His absolute permission. Hang all your hopes solely upon the One who distributes mercy to whomever He wills.

There is not a single moving creature on earth except that Allah guarantees its provision. Be at peace in your efforts, for the provision destined for you will never be confused with another's, let alone get lost.
The sincere intention to bring about reform (Ishlah) must always be accompanied by the belief that success and divine ability (Tawfiq) come solely from Allah, not from our own greatness.
The heaviest command for a servant is Istiqamah (remaining steadfast upon the path of truth). It requires guarding the heart from transgression and leaning towards injustice.
Be patient in obedience and in restraining yourself from sins. Indeed, Allah will never let the reward of the doers of good (Muhsinin) go to waste, no matter how small.
Worship and reliance (Tawakkul) are the two wings of faith. To Him all affairs are returned, so worship Him purely and lean your heart entirely upon Him alone.
Beautiful patience (Shabrun Jamil) is being patient without complaining to the creation. Yielding all the pain of a trial solely to Allah is the ultimate firmness of a believer's heart.
Allah is always in control of all affairs, turning the plots of enemies into mercy for His pious servants. However, human intellect is often too narrow to comprehend it in the beginning.
The soul is always inclined to command evil. Salvation from sin is not due to one's own greatness in resisting temptation, but purely because of the mercy and protection of Allah.
Complaining of deep pain and sorrow ONLY to Allah does not tarnish patience. On the contrary, that is the truest manifestation of Tawhid and servitude when the world feels constricting.

No matter how crushing the weight of a calamity is, a believer is forbidden to despair of Allah's mercy. Despair is, in essence, the loss of good thoughts (Husn al-Zhann) towards the Most Merciful Lord.

The change of destiny towards goodness always begins from within. As long as a servant strives to tread the path of obedience, Allah will open the doors of divine success (Tawfiq) to change their condition for the better.
The truth (Al-Haqq) is like clear water that settles and provides benefit, while falsehood is like dirty foam that floats briefly and will inevitably vanish, swallowed by time.
Patience in this world in obeying Allah may sometimes feel bitter, but later in Paradise, it will bear sweet fruits in the form of welcoming greetings of peace from the angels from every gate.

True inner peace is impossible to find in the glitter of the worldly life. That tranquility is only sent down into the hearts that are constantly moistened by Dhikr and the remembrance of the Lord of the worlds.
Allah is All-Powerful to erase or confirm destiny according to His will and wisdom. Therefore, never tire of supplicating, for du'a is a magnificent act of worship in His sight.

Gratitude (Shukr) is not merely a verbal utterance, but an acknowledgment of the heart and the utilization of blessings for obedience. This is the binder of blessings so they do not vanish, as well as the opener of doors for additional bounties.
The word of Tawhid is likened to a good tree; its roots (conviction) are firmly planted in the heart, and its branches (righteous deeds) reach up to the sky, providing goodness at all times.

Often, we fail to be grateful because we are too busy lamenting over what we do not yet possess. Yet, if we tried to count just a fraction of the blessings of health and oxygen, we would certainly be unable to enumerate them.
The supplication of Prophet Ibrahim teaches us not only to prioritize our own piety but also to continuously pray that our offspring's hearts remain steadfast in establishing the prayer.

Never think that Allah is unaware of the actions of the wrongdoers. The delay of punishment in this world is not a sign of His pleasure, but an Istidraj (a gradual drawing toward ruin) until the day of futile regret arrives.
The Quran is a holy book whose purity is guarded directly by Allah from all distortions until the Day of Judgment. This is an absolute guarantee not possessed by the scriptures of previous nations.
A servant must realize that Allah's mercy and forgiveness are as vast as the ocean for those who repent, yet His punishment is exceptionally severe for those who take sins lightly.
Do not let your eyes gaze longingly at the worldly luxuries enjoyed by the heedless. The guidance of the Quran and faith are sufficient as the greatest wealth that brings peace to your heart.
Worshipping Allah is not a seasonal activity. The ultimate finish line for a believer's obedience in this world is only when the angel of death arrives to take their soul.
Every single blessing, from the beating of our hearts to the provision we taste, originates purely from Allah alone. Why then do some people place their hopes in other than Him?

The promise of a good life (Hayatan Thayyibah) in this world, in the form of peace of mind, contentment (Qana'ah), and blessed provision, is the guaranteed reward for anyone who combines faith and righteous deeds.
A nation or a family that was initially prosperous can have its blessings instantly revoked by Allah and replaced with hunger and fear, as a consequence of being ungrateful for His favors.
Da'wah (calling to Islam) must be founded upon knowledge, wisdom, and good words. However, gentleness in preaching does not mean hiding the truth or compromising with falsehood.
Justice is to retaliate proportionately to the harm done. However, being patient and forgiving is the path of the magnanimous, which is far more superior and noble in the sight of Allah.
The special support and accompaniment (Ma'iyyah) of Allah is always with His pious servants and those who constantly beautify their deeds (Muhsinin) with sincerity.
Lowering oneself in humility before one's parents is not a disgrace, but rather an act of profound worship whose status sits right after the command to single out Allah in worship.
The perfect Sharia of Islam not only forbids vile acts like adultery but also firmly shuts all doors, gazes, and avenues leading near it in order to protect one's honor.
Do not walk upon the earth with arrogance. Indeed, you will never be able to tear the earth apart, nor will you ever reach the mountains in height. Humility is the true garment of a servant.
The Quran was revealed as a cure (Syifa') for the diseases of doubt in the intellect and desires in the heart, and it serves as a vast mercy for those who affirm it with faith.
Human intellect has very narrow limits. Questioning the deep essence of the soul only reveals how little portion of knowledge Allah has entrusted to us.
The youth of the Cave (Ashabul Kahf) saved their Tawhid by isolating themselves from a corrupt environment. Their prayer seeking mercy and guidance was their strongest shield amidst trials.
Do not speak with certainty about a future plan without linking it to the will of Allah (Insha'Allah), because we possess absolutely no power over what happens tomorrow.
Discipline yourself to continuously sit and associate with those who sincerely worship. Do not turn your attention away from them merely to pursue heedless worldly companionships.

Wealth and children are but the fleeting adornments of this world. As for the enduring righteous deeds (Al-Baqiyatush Shalihat), they are far better in reward and more excellent in hope with your Lord.

The journey of Prophet Musa to meet Khidr teaches an extraordinary adab (etiquette) of humility from a Messenger of Ulul Azmi in seeking knowledge, and serves as a reminder that above every person of knowledge, there is One who knows more.
Pray to Allah with a soft voice and a heart full of submission. The silence of the night is the best witness to the cries of a servant who genuinely needs his Lord.

Throughout his life, a believer never despairs in supplication. The phrase 'and I have never been disappointed in my prayer to You' is proof of an unshakable good expectation (Husn al-Zhann) towards Allah.
Be a person who brings blessings wherever you are. True blessing is when your presence constantly reminds others of obedience, knowledge, and goodness.
The beginning of the destruction of a generation is when they start to belittle and abandon the prayer, and then drown in following their worldly desires. The end of that road is clear misguidance.

Love and affection among people cannot be bought with wealth. However, for whoever cultivates faith and righteous deeds, Allah Himself will instill love and acceptance for them in the hearts of the earth's inhabitants.
The Quran was not revealed to make you miserable or suffer under rules. Rather, it was revealed as a mercy and a shining reminder so that your heart may be filled with expansiveness.

Before asking for your affairs to be made easy, ask for your chest to be expanded. Because a massive problem will feel light for an expanded chest, yet a trivial matter will feel suffocating for a constricted chest.
No matter how wicked the person being preached to is, convey the truth with gentle words. Gentleness is more likely to knock on the doors of guidance and remove arrogance from the listener's heart.
The only thing in this world that Allah commanded His Prophet to ask for an increase in is (beneficial) knowledge, not wealth, throne, or any other worldly glitter.

Do not let your eyes widen gazing at the blessings and luxuries Allah has bestowed upon others. The bounty of your Lord in the form of guidance and halal provision—even if little—is far better and more enduring.
The time of humanity's reckoning is drawing near, yet worldly daydreams often make the heart heedless and turn away from preparing for the inevitable Hereafter.
Every soul shall taste death. Know that Allah tests His servants not only with hardship and calamities, but also with ease and wealth as a trial (Fitnah).

Illness and the loss of wealth do not humiliate a servant in the eyes of Allah. The prayer of Prophet Ayyub teaches that acknowledging Allah's mercy amidst calamity is the key to having the trial lifted.

There is no savior from the darkness of suffering other than the purification of Tawhid and the admission of one's own wrongdoings. The prayer of Prophet Yunus (La ilaha illa anta...) is the master key that unlocks the greatest way out.
The traits of servants whose prayers are quickly answered are those who hasten to do good, constantly pray with a mix of profound hope and fear, and humble their hearts solely to Him.
The convulsion of the Day of Judgment is a terrifyingly immense thing. Piety built upon knowledge and righteous deeds in this world is the only fortress of salvation on that dreadful day.

Do not worship Allah on the 'edge of a cliff'. Do not be a servant who is only obedient when their provision is vast, but instantly turns away and harbors ill thoughts towards Allah when trials approach.

In every legislation of sacrifice (such as Qurbani), it is not the meat or the blood that reaches the pleasure of Allah, but the piety, the sincerity of intention, and the submission of the heart that accompanies it.
The most real and destructive blindness is not the loss of physical eyesight on the face, but the blindness of the inner eye (the heart) within the chest from accepting the light of truth.
The religion of Islam is built upon ease, not to cause hardship for His servants. Bow, prostrate, and do good solely for the sake of Allah; inevitably, you will achieve clear victory.
True victory and ultimate success have been guaranteed for the believers who begin the foundation of their deeds with Khushu' (deep humility) and submission of the heart in their prayers.
A trait of the perfection of a person's faith is their ability to restrain their tongue and limbs from words and actions that bring no benefit to their religious or worldly affairs.
Guarding one's honor from forbidden desires is the gateway to the purity of the soul. Violating these boundaries only plunges one into humiliation and a state of transgression.

A true believer is not one who sins and feels secure, but rather one who actively performs righteous deeds yet their heart still trembles with fear that their deeds might not be accepted by their Lord.
Repel the evil of people with that which is best. Patience in facing insults and returning them with kindness is the key to extinguishing the fire of enmity from ignorant tongues.
Lowering the gaze from what is forbidden is the first step to purifying the heart. From a guarded gaze, noble honor and true tranquility of the soul are born.
Repent all of you to Allah, O you who believe. No matter how many righteous deeds you have, you will always be in need of His forgiveness to achieve eternal success.

Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth. His guidance firmly planted in the chest of a believer is like a brightly lit lamp that never extinguishes, even when battered by storms of doubts and desires.

The busyness of commerce and the glitter of worldly wealth will never be able to distract the hearts of true men from remembering Allah, establishing the prayer with humility, and paying the obligatory charity (Zakat).
The sole principle of the believers when called to obey the laws of Allah and His Messenger ﷺ is to say, 'We hear and we obey.' Therein lies perfect salvation.

The servants of the Most Merciful (Ibadurrahman) are those who walk upon the earth with humility. And when the ignorant address them with insults, they respond with words of peace and turn away from futile arguments.

The trait of a servant beloved by Allah is one who enlivens the last third of their night. While people are fast asleep in the cradle of dreams, they are busy prostrating and standing, conversing intimately and humbly with their Lord.
Moderation is the pillar of goodness in managing wealth. A believer is neither stingy to the point of withholding the rights of others, nor extravagant in squandering wealth on futilities.

How vast is Allah's mercy; for those who offer sincere repentance (Taubat Nasuha), believe, and do righteous deeds, Allah not only forgives their sins but also replaces their pile of past misdeeds with the reward of good deeds.

The pinnacle of a family's happiness is when the husband, wife, and children become the coolness of the eyes due to their obedience to Allah. That is the vision of the servants of Ar-Rahman, praying to be made leaders for the righteous.

Absolute conviction in Allah during a crisis is exemplified by Prophet Musa. When the enemy surrounded him from behind and the sea stretched out before him, he remained steadfast, declaring: 'By no means! Indeed, my Lord is with me; He will guide me to a way out.'

The perfection of Prophet Ibrahim's Tawhid is seen in his adab (etiquette) towards Allah. He attributed creation, guidance, provision, and healing purely to Allah, while illness was attributed to his own weakness.

On the Day of Judgment, abundant piles of wealth and proud lineage will not provide even a speck of benefit. Absolute salvation belongs only to those who come to Allah with a sound heart (Qalbun Salim), free from the stain of shirk.
True sincerity in da'wah is refusing to expect material rewards or the praise of men. The pleasure and reward from the Lord of the worlds are sufficient as the sole purpose of the struggle.

Da'wah and the reformation of the Ummah must always begin from the closest circle. Saving one's immediate family from the threat of Hellfire is the primary obligation before going far to warn the wider society.

Power and wealth are not signs of absolute nobility, but rather a test of gratitude. Prophet Sulayman exemplified that the pinnacle of true success is when we humble worldly achievements by saying: 'This is by the grace of my Lord...'
Only Allah alone answers the prayer of the distressed when they call upon Him, and it is He Who removes the hardship. There is no true refuge other than Him when the doors of the earth seem closed.
The keys to the unseen in the heavens and the earth belong only to Allah. Claiming to know the future through fortune-telling or astrology is a very clear form of nullifying Tawhid.
Put your complete trust in Allah when you are upon the manifest truth. Allah's care always accompanies His servant who believes in the straightness of the path of the Quran and Sunnah.

Allah's promise is definitely true, but most people do not understand it. The story of Prophet Musa's mother teaches that whatever we entrust to Allah's care with reliance (Tawakkul) will certainly return bringing multiplied goodness.
A true believer is not one who never makes a mistake, but one who, when slipping into wrongdoing, immediately acknowledges their weakness and begs their Lord for forgiveness before death arrives.

In the midst of alienation and hunger, Prophet Musa offered the most beautiful prayer: 'My Lord, indeed I am, for whatever good You would send down to me, in need.' Showing weakness and pure need to Allah is the essence of worship.

The guidance of Tawfiq is purely in the Hands of Allah. You will not be able to force guidance even upon the person you love the most. Our duty is only to convey the message and pray for their goodness.
Use the blessings of your wealth and position to build a palace in the Hereafter, but do not forget your lawful portion in this world. Do good to others, just as Allah has done good to you.

Conceit (Ujub) is the disease that destroyed Qarun. Believing that wealth and success are achieved purely due to one's own brilliance—without acknowledging Allah's intervention—is the gateway to destruction.

Do people think they will be left to say, 'We believe,' without being tested? Trials are a just divine sieve to distinguish which faith is honestly planted in the heart, and which is merely a claim on the tongue.

Obedience to parents is a magnificent obligation, unless they command you to commit shirk. There is no obedience to the creation in disobeying the Creator, but continue to accompany them in this world with excellent character.

Taking protectors and reliances other than Allah is like taking shelter under a spider's web. It appears to have substance, but in reality, it is the most fragile defense, unable to withstand the crushing blows of worldly and otherworldly trials.

Establish the prayer with a present, humble heart. Indeed, the prayer established according to the Sunnah and with full understanding will undoubtedly become a shield that prevents its doer from immorality and wrongdoing.

The life of this world is nothing more than fleeting amusement and play, bound to vanish in time. People of sound intellect are those who actively build the deeds of Tawhid for the life of the eternal Hereafter.

Mawaddah (love) and rahmah (mercy) in marriage are the decrees and gifts of Allah that descend into His servants' hearts. They are not merely the result of human effort, but a sign of His greatness that demands gratitude so the household may feel tranquil.

Direct your face upright towards Islam. That is the pure natural disposition (Fitrah) that Allah has instilled in every human since birth. Turning away from Tawhid to shirk means violating and destroying the purity of one's own nature.

Wealth rolled out through usury (Riba) to multiply in the eyes of people, in reality, has no value and does not increase in the sight of Allah. However, zakat and charity given sincerely seeking the Face of Allah, that is what will be multiplied manifold.

Corruption has appeared on land and sea as a result of what human hands have earned. Various disasters, plagues, and natural destruction are merciful reprimands from Allah so that humans may taste the consequences of their sins, and then promptly repent and return to the path of obedience.

So be patient in treading the path of da'wah; indeed, the promise of Allah regarding the coming of help is true. Do not let the ignorant who do not believe in the truth shake the firmness of your heart in the slightest.

The greatest and most unforgivable injustice is not robbing people's wealth, but associating partners with Allah (shirk). That was Luqman's first testament of Tawhid to his son before advising on matters of character.

Dutifulness to parents is an absolute obligation right after singling out Allah in worship. However, if they force you to disobey, refuse gently and continue to accompany them in this world with perfect etiquette.

No matter how small a good deed or a sin is, even if it is hidden within a pitch-black rock in the depths of the earth, Allah will surely bring it forth on the Day of Judgment. This awareness is the foundation of fearing Him.
Establish the prayer, preach with knowledge, and be patient over whatever trials afflict you because of it. These three are monumental matters that are highly mandated and require strong resolve.
Do not turn your cheek away from people in arrogance, and do not walk on the earth with insolence. Indeed, Allah severely despises every self-deluded and boastful person.
The trait of true believers is that when the verses of Allah are recited, their hearts tremble and their bodies instantly fall in prostration, exalting their Lord while casting away all arrogance.

Their sides forsake their beds in the silence of the night to commune with their Lord in profound fear and hope. The night prayer (Tahajjud) is the school of sincerity and the hallmark of a servant yearning for Paradise.

No soul can ever imagine the beauty of the delights of Paradise that Allah has kept hidden for them. That is the perfect reward for the righteous deeds they kept secret from the eyes of people in this world.

It is impossible to equate the rank of a believer who is obedient with a transgressor who is prone to sin. The scale of Allah's justice dictates that the ultimate destination of both will never be the same.

Calamities, illnesses, and hardships in this worldly life are often inflicted by Allah as a form of His mercy, so that His servants may awaken from heedlessness and quickly repent before the arrival of the true torment of the Hereafter.
Allah is sufficient as a Protector and a place of reliance. When a person fully trusts in Him (Tawakkul), no enemy's plot or worldly difficulty can ever shatter the firmness of their heart.

The Messenger of Allah ﷺ is the scale of truth and the perfect role model. Following his footsteps and Sunnah in times of hardship and ease is the concrete proof of a servant's hope in Allah and the Last Day.

Nobility in the sight of Allah is not measured by gender or appearance, but by how steadfast one's Islam, submission, patience, and charity are. For these obedient souls, Allah has prepared forgiveness and a magnificent reward.

The legislation of the Hijab is not a restriction, but rather a manifestation of Allah's honoring and safeguarding of the believing women's dignity, so they may be recognized for their nobility and protected from the harm of the tongues and hands of the wicked.

Honesty of the tongue (Qaulan Sadida) is the key that opens the door of divine success (Tawfiq). Whoever consistently guards the truthfulness of their words out of fear of Allah, He will certainly rectify their deeds and forgive their sins.

Nothing escapes Allah's knowledge, not even the weight of an atom hidden in the heavens and the earth. The awareness of His all-encompassing knowledge should bear the fruit of Muraqabah (the feeling of being watched) in every second of our lives.

True gratitude is not enough with just the utterance of 'Alhamdulillah'. Complete gratitude is mobilizing all of one's limbs and the received blessings to perform righteous deeds and obey the Bestower of Blessings.

The story of the people of Saba' is an eternal warning. No matter how great a nation's prosperity and how advanced its civilization, if its inhabitants turn away from piety, those blessings can be instantly revoked and replaced with destruction.

The abundance of wealth and offspring is by no means a parameter of a person's closeness to Allah. The nobility of rank in His sight is purely measured by the purity of Tawhid and the quality of righteous deeds performed with sincerity.
Never fear poverty due to spending in the way of Allah. Every wealth given in charity with the right intention will certainly be replaced with something far more blessed by Allah, the Best Provider.
Whatever mercy Allah grants to mankind, none can withhold it. This conviction is the most potent medicine to cure the heart from the diseases of envy and despair.

Allah's promise regarding the Day of Resurrection is an absolute certainty. So let not the glitter of this world deceive you, and let not the master deceiver (Satan) deceive you concerning obedience to your Lord.

O mankind, in reality, you are all deeply impoverished (faqir) and in desperate need of Allah in every single breath. Meanwhile, Allah is the Free of need (Al-Ghaniyy) and absolutely does not need the obedience of His servants.
True knowledge is not measured merely by how much one has memorized or the breadth of their insight, but by how much fear (Khasyyah) grows in the heart towards Allah, the Most Great.
Reciting the Quran, establishing the prayer, and giving charity secretly and openly is a spiritual trade that will never suffer a loss. In it lies an infinite addition of grace.

The reminder of the Quran will only bear sweet fruit and benefit those who possess a submissive heart to follow the guidance, and who harbor fear of Ar-Rahman (The Most Merciful) even in unseen solitude.

A sincere caller to the truth holds no grudges. Even after his people killed him, he instead hoped, 'Ah, if only my people knew of Allah's forgiveness for me.' That is the pinnacle of compassion in da'wah.

The precise orbits of the sun and the moon, never overtaking one another, are manifest proofs of Allah's power. It is impossible for this incredibly precise universe to be created without a Most Regulating Creator.

A time will come when the tongue will be tightly sealed. The hands that acted will speak, and the feet that walked will testify. There will be no more room to lie and hide sins on the Day of Judgment.
For Allah, resurrecting billions of humans from death is an exceptionally light matter. When He intends a thing, He only says to it, 'Be,' (Kun) and instantly, it is.

Every step and deed of ours in this world will be paused for a moment in the Gathering Place (Mahsyar) to be held accountable. Not a single intention or action can escape the questioning of Allah's justice.

Salvation before Allah on that Day can only be attained by bringing a 'Qalbun Salim' (a sound heart), which is a heart secure from the stains of shirk, doubt, and clean from any inclination towards disobedience.

The highest education of Tawhid is seen when the son responded to the immensely heavy command of the sacrifice test with full obedience: 'O my father, do as you are commanded.' A child's obedience is born from the parents' exemplary Tawhid.

The acts of obedience that we consistently maintain during times of ease will eventually become our main saviors when we are squeezed by hardship, just as the dhikr and tasbih of Prophet Yunus saved him from the belly of the whale.

The victory of Allah's servants and His messengers is an absolute decree. If not in this world in the form of triumph, then certainly in the form of the victory of proof (the truth) and the reward of eternal salvation in the Hereafter.

Facing the insults and mockery of the ignorant requires the shield of patience. And the best fortifier of patience is to multiply dhikr and emulate the righteousness of Allah's pious servants of the past.

The noble ones in the sight of Allah are not those who never err, but those who, upon realizing their mistakes, immediately fall down seeking forgiveness (Inabah) without delay, let alone seeking justification.

The primary purpose of the revelation of this blessed Quran is not merely for its letters to be recited hastily, but for its meanings to be deeply pondered over (tadabbur) and its contents to be practiced by people of understanding.

The highest praise from Allah for His servant is the title 'an excellent servant, oft-returning (in repentance).' This title was bestowed upon Prophet Ayyub for his absolute patience in facing the trials of severe illness and loss of wealth.
Arrogance was the first sin used to disobey Allah in the heavens. Rejecting the Sharia by relying on pure logic and feeling superior to others is to inherit the fundamental trait of Iblis (Satan).
The pinnacle of a deed's acceptance is when it is performed with pure devotion (Ikhlas) solely for Allah, cleansed from all stains of shirk and showing-off (Riya') within the heart.

The best of statements is the Quran. Its verses can make the skins shiver and soften the hardened hearts of those who fear their Lord, then guiding them toward the tranquility of the remembrance of Allah.
Death is an absolute certainty awaiting every creature. All disputes and injustices left unresolved in this world will eventually be judged with absolute fairness before the Divine Court.

When enemies try to frighten you with the power of creation, always remember His word: 'Is not Allah sufficient for His servant?' This conviction is the strongest shield of reliance (Tawakkul) for the soul of a believer.

No matter how enormous and dark the pile of your past sins is, you are forbidden to despair of Allah's mercy. Indeed, the door of His forgiveness is never closed to a servant who sincerely takes steps to return and repent.

Allah is the Forgiver of sin and Accepter of repentance, yet His punishment is extremely severe for those who defy Him. Balancing hope (Raja') and fear (Khauf) within the chest is the axis of faith's salvation.

How noble is the status of those who believe and repent; even the angels who bear the Throne unceasingly pray for their forgiveness and for them and their righteous families to be admitted into the protective Paradise.
Do not be deceived by the glamorous power and wealth of the disbelievers on earth. The life of this world is but a fleeting transit, whereas the Hereafter is the true realm of eternity.

Supplication (Du'a) is not merely a request for needs, but the very essence of worship. Refusing to pray and feeling no need for Allah is a form of arrogance threatened with eternal humiliation in Hellfire.

Faith and declarations of repentance uttered when Allah's punishment has already descended and swept before one's eyes will no longer bring any benefit. The best time to return to Him is now, while you are still breathing in times of ease.

Steadfast faith (Istiqamah) maintained until the end of life will bear sweet fruit. Angels will descend upon that soul with glad tidings: 'Fear not, nor grieve, but receive the good news of Paradise.'
There is no profession or speech more noble on this earth than the tongue of a caller to the path of Allah (Da'wah), who proves his sincerity with righteous deeds and a sense of pride as a Muslim.

Repel evil with that which is perfectly good. That is the character of Allah's messengers, which is capable of transforming a bitter, hate-filled enemy into what seems like an intimately loyal friend.
Whenever anger burns the intellect or the temptation to sin whispers in the chest, immediately seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing of every unseen plot of Satan.
The Quran is a guiding path and a healing cure for diseases of the heart and doubts. However, this healing light will only permeate and work optimally within the chests of the believers.

The grand principle in knowing Allah is believing in His greatness: 'There is nothing like unto Him.' Affirming His attributes without likening Him to the creation is the pinnacle of the orthodox creed.

Whoever makes the Hereafter the field of their pursuit, Allah will multiply their harvest. But whoever's ambition is purely for this world, Allah gives them a small portion of it, but they will have no share whatsoever in the Hereafter.

Every calamity, hardship, and sorrow that afflicts us is actually the fruit of the sins committed by our own hands. And know that the wrongdoings Allah pardons and conceals from us are far more numerous than what He punishes us for.

The trait of a blessed society is those who establish prayer and resolve all their important affairs through mutual consultation (Shura). Decisions deliberated upon with knowledge will always lead to blessings.
Justice is retaliating against evil proportionately. However, whoever chooses to forgive and reconcile to mend the ties of brotherhood, their reward rests directly upon Allah, the Most Noble.

Allah's mercy in the form of faith, guidance, and the Quran is far more precious than all worldly adornments. The disparity of provision on earth is a divine test so humans may cooperate with one another, not to boast against each other.

Were it not for the fear that all mankind would simultaneously become disbelievers, Allah would have provided abundant silver and gold palaces for His opposers. This is proof of how utterly worthless this world is in the sight of Allah.

The heaviest punishment in this world is not poverty, but when someone turns away from the Quran, and Allah then assigns a devil as their intimate companion who constantly justifies their misguidance as if they were on the right path.

Hold fast to the Sunnah and revelation amidst the storms of trials (Fitnah) in the end times. That is the only rope of salvation that guarantees your steps remain upon the straight path (Sirat al-Mustaqim).
Every friendship built upon disobedience and mere worldly interests will turn into bitter enmity on the Day of Judgment. The only true, eternal friendship is one founded upon piety (Taqwa).

The Night of Decree (Lailatul Qadr) is the witness to the revelation of the Quran. On that blessed night, every destiny, provision, and lifespan for the coming year is detailed with absolute justice by Allah's will.

Only Allah holds absolute control over life and death. The realization that He is our Lord and the Lord of our forefathers should be able to sever all forms of shirk from within the depths of our hearts.

The heavens, the earth, and all that is within them were not created merely for vanity or play. There is a profound truth and a great day of accountability awaiting behind the creation of this universe.

A day will come when relations, lineage, and loyal friends will no longer be able to provide the slightest benefit of protection. On that Day of Recompense, the only shade is the mercy of Allah, the Exalted in Might.
Allah has made the Quran easy to understand as a lesson and a reminder. Therefore, truly at a loss is the soul that refuses to take wisdom and lets its heart turn to stone against the clear guidance.

Allah has subjected whatever is in the heavens and the earth for the benefit of mankind. Reflecting upon this immense blessing should give birth to deep submission and gratitude in the hearts of those who reflect.
Whoever does a righteous deed, its reward is purely for his own soul, and whoever commits a sin, its evil consequence will fall upon him. Allah's justice is absolute; not a single servant is wronged.
The Islamic Sharia is a brightly illuminated path. Follow the guidance of this revelation steadfastly, and do not ever yield to the desires of the ignorant who possess no knowledge of the truth.

It is impossible to equate the life and death of those actively performing righteous deeds with those who love committing sins. The scales of Divine justice will never mix the light of obedience with the darkness of disobedience.

The most destructive disease of the heart is taking one's ego and desires as an 'object of worship.' When the truth is rejected due to arrogance, Allah seals their hearing and inner sight completely from guidance.

The key to inner peace lies in two things: the declaration of Tawhid 'Our Lord is Allah', followed by steadfastness (Istiqamah) upon the Sunnah. For them, there is no fear in facing tomorrow and no grief in mourning the past.

Dutifulness to parents is a heavenly testament. Remember how your mother bore you and gave birth to you in multiplied hardships. Pray for their forgiveness as a manifestation of your gratitude to the Creator.

Every human will occupy varying degrees in the Hereafter according to the quality of their sincerity in their worldly deeds. Allah will pay them in full for their deeds without wronging or diminishing their goodness in the slightest.
Respond to the caller of Allah and believe in Him. The guiding grace (Tawfiq) welcomed with faith will become an expiation for past sins and a sturdy protector from a painful agony.

Be patient in facing the harm of people, just as the Messengers of Firm Resolve (Ulul Azmi) were patient. Do not hasten to seek revenge, because the life of this world is truly very short, like a mere hour of a day.

The good deeds of those who oppose the Sharia will vanish, holding no value in the sight of Allah. Conversely, faith in what was revealed to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ is an eraser of sins and a bringer of improved conditions in this world and the Hereafter.

Allah's help is conditional. Whoever supports the religion of Allah by upholding Tawhid and defending the Sunnah of His Messenger, Allah will certainly support them and plant their feet firmly when facing the enemy.

Knowledge must precede words and actions. Know with absolute certainty of knowledge that there is no deity worthy of worship in truth except Allah, and only then ask for forgiveness for your sins and the sins of the believing men and women.

The locking of the heart from guidance often originates from a reluctance to ponder (tadabbur) over the verses of the Quran. A heart enclosed by the glare of lusts will not be able to receive the reflection of the light of guidance.

Being stingy in spending in the way of Allah is, in reality, wronging oneself. Allah is Free of need and does not require our wealth at all; rather, we are the destitute who desperately need His rewards in the Hereafter.

Manifest victory often comes in forms unexpected by human intellect. The Treaty of Hudaibiyah, which initially felt disadvantageous, actually became the gateway for the spread of da'wah and a massive victory for the Muslims.

Inner tranquility (Sakinah) is an army of Allah sent down into the hearts of the believers when facing critical moments. From this tranquility, their faith grows stronger and remains unshaken by trials.
Absolute obedience to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ is a manifestation of obedience to Allah. Denying his Sunnah is tantamount to breaching the covenant of faith that a servant has pledged.
Islam is the religion of truth and will definitely be made victorious by Allah over all misguidance. Allah's promise of protection for this religion is a certainty that will never fail.

The character of the best generation of this Ummah (the Companions) is being forceful against disbelief but full of mercy among fellow believers. Their faces shine with the marks of prostration, purely out of sincerity in seeking Allah's pleasure.
The highest etiquette of a believer is absolute submission to the Sharia. Never precede the decree of Allah and His Messenger ﷺ with opinions, traditions, or mere intellect.
Hastening to believe and spread news from a wicked person is the gateway to regret and injustice. Tabayyun (clarification) is the Ummah's shield against slanders that destroy brotherhood.
The brotherhood of faith is stronger than blood ties. If a dispute arises between your brothers, be a just peacemaker, not an instigator who pours fire on the embers of enmity.
Do not belittle others because of their physical appearance, wealth, or past. It could be that those you mock in this world hold a status and piety far nobler in the sight of Allah.
Backbiting (Ghibah) is like eating the dead flesh of your own brother. Avoid negative assumptions and tajassus (spying to find faults), for the honor of a Muslim is highly sacred to their Lord.

Not a single inner whisper is hidden from Allah. His knowledge is closer to man than his own jugular vein. This awareness is the primary foundation of the trait of muraqabah (feeling constantly watched by Allah).
Every uttered word, whether a joke or in anger, will not escape the recording of the watching angels (Raqib and 'Atid). An unguarded tongue is the fastest route to regret on the Day of Reckoning.
The agony of death (Sakaratul Maut) will inevitably arrive bringing the truth that man has always avoided. At that second, all veils are lifted, and there is nowhere to run from the divine decree.
Paradise is brought near for the righteous; namely, those who constantly turn back in repentance (Awwab), preserve His limits, and fear Ar-Rahman even in unseen solitude.

The advice and reminder of the Quran will only vibrate and come alive within a heart that is fully attentive; a soul that earnestly listens to the truth without being distracted by worldly heedlessness.

On this earth, and also within yourselves, are signs of Allah's greatness for people of certain faith. Do you not observe how magnificent the creation of your body and intellect is, which guides you to Tawhid?

Your provision and all that you are promised have already been decreed in the heavens. So do not let your heart be anxious over guaranteed worldly matters, causing you to neglect your obligations to the Provider.
Flee towards Allah. Leave ignorance for knowledge, disobedience for obedience, and heedlessness for dhikr. That is the essence of Hijrah and the safest refuge for your soul.
Never tire of giving good advice. Even if some reject it or turn away, indeed, religious reminders will certainly provide benefit and firmness to the hearts of the believers.
The sole purpose for the creation of jinn and mankind is only to purify their worship (Tawhid) for Allah alone. Any worldly busyness that diverts from this magnificent purpose is a sheer vanity.
The decree of Allah's punishment for those who deny the truth is a certainty that cannot be repelled by anyone. This is a stern warning for mankind to promptly repent before it is too late.
Among Allah's honors for believing parents is elevating the ranks of their offspring in Paradise to be equal to theirs, as a completion of family happiness in the eternal abode.

The fear (Khasyyah) of Allah's punishment while gathered with family in this world actually becomes the cause for the arrival of safety and the grace of Allah's protection from the torment of Hell in the Hereafter.

It is impossible for humans to be created out of nothing without a Creator, and it is equally impossible that they created this universe themselves. A sound mind reflecting upon this will surely lead to the acknowledgment of Allah's Oneness.
Be patient with the decree of your Lord and the harm from people in da'wah. Indeed, you are always under Our observation and protection. This conviction is the greatest comforter for a believer.

Every word and guidance of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in religious matters does not originate from his own desires or intellect, but is pure revelation sent down by Allah. Obedience to the Sunnah is obedience to the Creator.

The peak of perfect etiquette of a servant was portrayed by the Messenger of Allah ﷺ during the Mi'raj. Amidst the grandeur of Allah's magnificent signs in the seventh heaven, his gaze remained steadfast, lowered in deep humility, never transgressing the permitted limit.

No person will bear the sin of another, and one will only reap the goodness of what they have sincerely strived for themselves. Do not anchor your Hereafter's salvation on the greatness of your lineage, but on the earnestness of your deeds of Tawhid.

It is Allah who creates the laughter of happiness and the tears of sorrow. When your chest feels suffocated by worldly pressures, return everything to the One who holds all emotions and spaciousness within the human heart.

A hardened heart will laugh at and neglect the warnings of the divine verses. Conversely, a living and believing heart will tremble and weep when reflecting upon Allah's threats and promises that are bound to happen.

The Hour is drawing near, and the signs of Allah's greatness have been displayed clearly. Yet, for souls whose hearts turn away and follow their desires, even a miracle as clear as the splitting of the moon will be denied as mere magic.

Allah has guaranteed the ease of the Quran to be recited, memorized, and its meanings understood. If anyone finds it difficult, it is not the verses that are heavy, but the willingness of the heart that has not fully submitted to take the lesson.

Not a single leaf falls, and not a single calamity strikes, except that its destiny has been written with absolute precision by Allah. Believing in destiny (Qadar) is a pillar of tranquility that will eliminate all complaints.

Every small step, spoken word, down to the sins we consider trivial, everything is neatly recorded without omission in the scrolls of deeds. This awareness demands us to constantly self-reflect before the Day of Weighing arrives.

For those who are pious and restrain themselves from forbidden worldly desires, Allah has prepared gardens of Paradise and a seat of truth and highly honored rank (Ma'qad Shidq) in the presence of the Sovereign, the Perfect in Ability. That is the peak of all victories.

The greatest mercy from Allah, the Most Compassionate, is not the glitter of worldly wealth, but the mercy of creating humans perfected with intellect, then teaching them the Quran as a guide for life and a manual for speaking uprightly.

The repetition of the verse 'So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?' is a sharp reprimand for jinn and mankind. True gratitude is born from the heart's acknowledgment that not a single cell in our body escapes His bounty.

All grandeur and every inhabitant on this earth will definitely perish. The awareness of the world's transience should melt away any arrogance in the chest, because ultimately, only the Face of Allah, the Majestic and Noble, will remain eternally.

Whoever, while alone, intends to commit a sin but restrains himself out of fear of the Day he will stand before his Lord, Allah promises him the reward of two gardens of Paradise of unparalleled beauty.

Excellence (Ihsan) in fulfilling the rights of Allah and the rights of the creation will never be rewarded by Allah except with an excellence that far surpasses it. That is the decree of the Most Generous, which is never broken.

The Day of Judgment is a magnificent event that will lower unjust rulers to the depths of Hell and elevate the pious servants to the highest ranks in Paradise. On that Day, all pride in lineage and wealth becomes worthless.

Those who are always at the forefront (As-Sabiqun) in welcoming obedience and hastening to repent from sins in this world, they will be the first group brought nearest to Allah, crowned with His pleasure.

The complex creation of humans from a despised drop of sperm is an absolute proof of Allah's power. If He was able to create you from nothingness into existence, it is exceedingly trivial for Him to resurrect you again from a pile of dust.

Do we not reflect, that a sip of fresh water that quenches our thirst falls from the clouds purely by Allah's mercy. If He willed to make it salty and bitter, no technology could stop it. Why, then, are these tongues reluctant to be grateful?

When the soul is thrust up to the collarbone, all ranks, wealth, and the aid of physicians will be unable to return it. At that second, man realizes his utter weakness, as the angel of death arrives bearing the divine decree.

The wealth you hold today is, in reality, merely a trust that Allah has placed under your authority. Spend from it, for charity based on faith will surely bear a massive reward in the sight of the True Owner.

On the Day of Judgment, the light of the hypocrites will be extinguished. They will beg for a glimmer of light from the believers, but will be answered: 'Go back to the world and seek light there.' The light of the Hereafter can only be bought with deeds of Tawhid in this world.

Has the time not come for the hearts of the believers to submit humbly in the remembrance of Allah? Do not let your heart turn to stone due to prolonged worldly hopes, like the previous nations who became heedless and rebellious.

Know that the life of this world is but amusement, adornment, and boasting of fleeting wealth, like greenery that soon turns yellow and crumbles. The Hereafter, however, is eternity; in it is severe punishment, or forgiveness and pleasure.

Every disaster on earth and within yourselves was already written in the Preserved Tablet (Lauhul Mahfuzh) before it occurred. That is so you may not overly grieve over what you miss, nor overly exult (in pride) over the blessings you attain.

There is no secret conversation between three people except that Allah is the fourth of them. His knowledge encompasses everything, wherever they may be, until the Day of Judgment when He will inform them of all those deeds.

When you converse secretly, do not conspire in sin, aggression, and disobedience to the Messenger ﷺ. Converse secretly in righteousness and piety. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing of the intentions behind every hidden conversation.

Maintaining etiquette and making room in gatherings of knowledge is a mandate of the Sharia. Allah will make provisions and affairs spacious for the servant who expansively makes room for his brother to hear goodness.

Allah raises the ranks of those who believe among you, and He elevates those who are given religious knowledge by several degrees. Islamic knowledge is the key to nobility in this world and salvation in the Hereafter.

You will not find a people who truly believe in Allah making close friends and granting loyalty (Wala') to the enemies of Allah and His Messenger, even if they were their own fathers, sons, or relatives.
O you who believe, fear Allah and let every soul reflect on what provisions of deeds it has put forward for tomorrow (the Hereafter), which is certain to come.

The heaviest punishment in this world for someone is not the loss of wealth, but the moment they forget Allah, and Allah consequently causes them to forget the salvation and welfare of their own souls.
Truly, the miserable inhabitants of Hellfire and the triumphant inhabitants of Paradise will never be equal. True victory belongs solely to those who succeed in stepping into Paradise.
If this Quran had been sent down upon a mountain, you would have seen that mighty mountain humbled and shattered to pieces out of intense fear of Allah. Why, then, do human hearts remain like stone?

He is Allah; there is no deity worthy of worship except Him. Knowing the majesty of His beautiful names (Asmaul Husna) is the shortest path to cultivating perfect love, hope, and submission in the heart of a servant.
Love for Allah and His Messenger requires a believer not to take the enemies of the religion as confidants and objects of loyalty, even if they reciprocate with pseudo-affection.
Ties of kinship and love for children will not benefit you in the slightest on the Day of Judgment if you sacrifice your piety to Allah merely to please them in this world.

Emulate the firmness of Prophet Ibrahim. They disassociated themselves with full conviction from the shirk of their people, pledging: 'Indeed, we are completely free from you and from whatever you worship besides Allah.'
Islam does not forbid you from doing good, acting justly, and maintaining ties with non-Muslims, as long as they do not fight you on account of your religion and do not drive you out of your homes.

The condition for establishing a faithful society begins with a commitment to abstain from shirk, theft, adultery, and falsehood, just as the pledge of allegiance (Bai'ah) of the believing women before the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.
A matter that incurs immense wrath in the sight of Allah is when your tongue is eloquent in advising goodness and promising deeds, yet your actions do not prove it in the slightest.
Allah truly loves His servants who fight to defend His religion in a well-organized rank, united and strengthening one another like a solidly structured building.

The disbelievers constantly strive to extinguish the light of Allah's guidance with the deceit of their tongues. But indeed, Allah will certainly perfect the light of His religion, even if they detest it.

Shall I guide you to the most profitable trade? It is to believe in Allah and His Messenger, and then to strive with your wealth and your lives. That is the investment that will save you from a painful punishment.
For the souls that sincerely sacrifice for His religion, Allah not only promises Paradise in the Hereafter, but also an immediate bounty in this world in the form of manifest help and a near victory.
All inhabitants of the heavens and the earth unceasingly glorify Allah. How arrogant is man if he, who is endowed with intellect and the most blessings, has the most reluctant tongue to remember Him.

The primary duty of an educator who emulates the Messenger of Allah ﷺ is to purify the souls of their students (moral cultivation) first, before teaching them the contents of the Book and the wisdom of knowledge.

A person who memorizes verses and Islamic laws but does not practice them is likened to a donkey carrying a load of thick books. It carries the burden of knowledge, but reaps absolutely no benefit from it.

When the call for Friday prayer echoes, leave behind the hustle and bustle of your world for a moment. Hasten towards the remembrance of Allah, for indeed, blessing lies in obedience, not in distracting commerce.

Do not let your eyes be blinded by the glitter of worldly amusement and profit when the call to obedience arrives. Say to your heart with conviction: 'The reward and pleasure with Allah are far better, and He is the Best of Providers.'

A charming physical appearance and captivating speech are of no value in the sight of Allah if the heart is void of faith. The hypocrites are likened to propped-up timbers; seemingly solid on the outside, yet stiff and meaningless on the inside.
Wealth and children are the adornments of this world. However, truly at a loss is whoever allows their love for both to turn them away and distract their heart from the remembrance of Allah.

Do not delay charity until the agony of death arrives. The greatest regret of a dying servant is not that they haven't had the chance to enjoy the world, but rather that they haven't had the chance to give charity and be among the righteous.

Death can never be delayed or advanced even for a second when its appointed time has arrived. The best time to save up good deeds is today, while your breath still flows and your body still has strength.

Allah knows all that is hidden in the heavens and the earth, even the darkest secrets in the recesses of your heart. Make this conviction the brake to restrain yourself from disobedience during moments of solitude.

The Day of Judgment is called 'Yawm at-Taghabun' (The Day of Mutual Loss and Gain). On that day, the sinners will lament their torment, and even the inhabitants of Paradise will regret why they did not perform more good deeds while in the world.
Not a single calamity strikes except by the permission of Allah. Whoever is pleased and believes in His decree, Allah will surely guide their heart towards an unshakable tranquility.

Sometimes the heaviest trial of faith comes from those closest to you; your spouses or your children. Pardon their shortcomings, yet remain vigilant so that your love for them does not overpower your obedience to Allah.
Indeed, your wealth and your children are but a trial (Fitnah). They are testing grounds to see whether you prioritize the rights of Allah, or if you instead drown in the love for creation.

Fear Allah as much as you are able. The Islamic Sharia does not demand perfection beyond the limits of human capability, but it demands earnest effort and pure intentions in every available avenue of obedience.
Whoever earnestly fears Allah in times of hardship as well as ease, Allah will undoubtedly open a way out for them from all the exhaustions of worldly and otherworldly affairs.
And Allah will provide for him from where he does not expect. Whoever relies completely upon Allah, Allah will certainly suffice all their needs, for His will is unmatched by anyone.
Piety (Taqwa) is the master key that opens the doors of ease. Whoever makes piety their garment, Allah will surely make all their seemingly complicated affairs easy and spacious.
Allah will never burden a servant except according to the capacity He has granted them. Rest assured, that Allah will surely bring about ease after hardship.

Do you not take a lesson from the many populations that rebelled against the command of their Lord, and were then held to a severe account? That is the evil consequence of the arrogance in rejecting the truth.
Do not forbid upon yourself what Allah has made lawful, merely to seek the pleasure of people—even if they are your spouses or closest ones. The decree of Allah stands above all feelings.

O you who believe, protect yourselves and your families from the Hellfire. The education of Tawhid and the cultivation of character within the household is the greatest responsibility before you set out to reform the world.
Repent to Allah with a sincere repentance (Taubat Nasuha). The hope of having sins forgiven and being admitted into Paradise begins with true remorse, where the misdeed is never repeated again.
Even the bond of marriage with a Prophet will not save someone in the Hereafter if they reject guidance. Allah's justice recompenses every soul purely based on its own faith and deeds.

Pharaoh's wife, Asiyah, teaches an extraordinary steadfastness of faith. Despite living under a tyrant drowning in wealth, her prayer was purely hoping for a house to be built in Paradise, proving that the world no longer dazzled her heart.

Allah created death and life to test which of you is best in deed (Ahsanu 'Amala). The conditions for the best deed are sincerity for Allah and correctness according to His Messenger's Sunnah, not merely the greatest in quantity.
Those who fear their Lord in unseen solitude, for them is forgiveness and a great reward. Fear in silence is the most tangible proof of honest faith.
It is Allah Who has made the earth subservient to you. Walk among its slopes and seek lawful provisions. But remember, unto Him you will eventually be resurrected to account for what you consumed.

Do they not observe the birds above them, spreading their wings and folding them? None holds them up in the sky except Ar-Rahman (The Most Merciful). The sensitivity to ponder the universe is the gateway to recognizing His greatness.
Allah has granted you hearing, sight, and hearts so that you may comprehend the truth. Yet ironically, how little do people use those blessings to be grateful.
And indeed, you (O Muhammad) are truly of a magnificent character. His character was the living translation of the Quran; a reflection of patience, gentleness, and perfect obedience to the Creator.

The story of the owners of the garden is an eternal warning. Their ill intention and stinginess to withhold the rights of the poor became the very cause of a torment that scorched all their wealth overnight.
A believer who slips into sin and immediately realizes it does not despair. They blame their desires, repent, and hope their Lord will grant them a better substitute for their remorse.

On the Day of Judgment, when the shin is uncovered and people are called to prostrate, the hypocrites will be unable to do so. Their backs will become stiff as a retribution for their reluctance to prostrate while in the world.

Be patient in facing the decree of your Lord and the harm of your people. Do not hastily give up like Prophet Yunus (the companion of the fish) who was struck by deep sorrow before the permission of Allah arrived.

The Day of Judgment is the day when all promised realities will vividly manifest. The destruction of 'Aad and Thamud is proof of how terrifying Allah's wrath is for those who deny the Day of Reckoning.
The peak of a believer's joy in the gathering of Mahsyar is when they receive their record of deeds from the right. With immense pride, they will call out to all of humanity: 'Here, read my record!'
The delights of Paradise, whose fruits hang low to be picked, are the reward for the hunger, fatigue, and thirst you endured for the sake of obeying Allah during your brief past in the world.
The most heart-wrenching scream of despair is when someone receives their record from the left. At that moment, the hoarded wealth and the boasted power are utterly shattered, leaving nothing behind.
The two main foundations that save humans from the chains of the blazing Hellfire are: purifying Tawhid for Allah the Most Great, and having a tangible concern to alleviate the hunger of the poor.

One day in the gathering place of Mahsyar measures equal to fifty thousand years of this world. Facing this terrifying certainty, be patient in this world with a beautiful patience, without complaining to the creation.
The basic nature of the human soul is highly fragile; it easily wails in panic when struck by hardship, yet instantly becomes exceedingly stingy when Allah expands the flow of provision for it.
The only cure for the diseases of complaining and stinginess is the prayer. Namely, those who not only perform the prayer but constantly guard their steadfastness and humility within it.
True believers realize that within a portion of their wealth, there is an absolute right that must be set aside for those who ask and those who are deprived yet restrain themselves.
Guarding one's honor from wild desires, fulfilling trusts, and keeping promises are not merely social etiquettes, but the primary tickets to eternal nobility in the gardens of Paradise.
The da'wah of Prophet Nuh teaches boundless resilience. He called his people day and night, secretly and openly, without ever feeling fatigued even as his nation kept running away from the truth.

Istighfar (seeking forgiveness) is the key that unlocks the doors of heaven. Seek forgiveness from your Lord, and He will send down abundant rain, multiply your wealth and children, and make your gardens and rivers flourish.
Why do humans hesitate to revere Allah as He deserves? Even though, if they reflect, Allah has created them from a mere drop of sperm into a perfect human being through marvelous stages of creation.
Blind fanaticism towards venerated figures (like Wadd, Suwa', Yaghuth) is an ancient disease. Elevating humans beyond their boundaries is the main gateway to shirk and manifest misguidance.

Prophet Nuh's prayer seeking forgiveness for himself, his parents, and all the believers is an exemplar of true love. The most beautiful kindness we can offer others is praying for their forgiveness when they are unaware.

The Quran possesses an extraordinary magnetism. Even a group of jinn who heard it were instantly amazed and believed, realizing that these verses guide to the path of truth, which is impossible to stem from human magic.
The jinn fully realized the essence of their servitude. They were certain that not a single creature on earth can escape Allah's power, and there is nowhere to run from His decree.

If humans and jinn were to remain steadfast upon the path of Tawhid, Allah would undoubtedly pour down upon them abundant provision (water). But know that even an expansion of provision is a test to see who is grateful.
Indeed, the mosques belong solely to Allah. So do not ever invoke or worship anyone within them alongside Allah. Purifying worship solely for Him is the condition for deeds to be accepted.

Knowledge of the unseen (Ghaib) is absolutely within the grasp of Allah. He does not reveal His unseen secrets to anyone in the heavens or on earth, except a small portion to the messengers He is pleased with, as a miracle of truth.

O you who wraps himself [in clothing], arise in the night for prayer. The silence of the night is the best school to fortify the soul before bearing the immensely heavy burden of the Sharia and da'wah during the day.

Arising at night for worship is the most appropriate time to subdue the desires. It is then that the heart and tongue are most easily united to comprehend the meanings of the Quranic recitation with profound humility.
Be patient over all the insults and mockery of those who oppose you. Leave them in a gracious manner, without harboring grudges, and let the justice of Allah deal with them later.
This religion is built upon ease. If you are exhausted, ill, or busy seeking provision, then recite what is easy for you of the Quran. But never abandon the obligatory prayers and zakat.
Whatever good you put forward for yourselves from now, you will certainly find it with Allah as a far better and greater reward on the Day of Judgment.
Arise and warn! The duty of a believer is not to remain silent in the comfort of worldly blankets, but to rise and call humanity to magnify Allah and purify their deeds from all stains of shirk.
Do not give something with a greedy intention to gain more in return from people. Purify your charity and gifts, and make your patience purely out of seeking the Face of your Lord.

Every soul is pledged and fully accountable for what it has earned in the world, except the companions of the right (Ashabul Yamin). They have freed themselves from the shackles of Hell through absolute obedience to Allah.

When the inhabitants of Hell are asked what drove them into Saqar, they will answer: 'We were not of those who prayed, nor did we feed the poor, and we used to indulge in vain talk with the heedless.'

The intercession of anyone will never be able to penetrate and save those whose hearts have turned to stone against the reminders of the Quran. They flee from the truth like frightened wild donkeys fleeing from a lion.

Allah swears by the 'Nafs Lawwamah', the soul of a believer that constantly blames and corrects itself for any shortcomings in obedience. A soul sensitive to its own sins is a highly noble soul in His sight.
Does man think that his bones, which have crumbled to dust, will not be gathered again? Indeed, Allah is All-Powerful to perfectly reassemble even the very tips of his fingers.

On the Day of Judgment, the faces of the believers will be radiant and glowing. The pinnacle of all the indescribable delights of Paradise is when they are permitted to gaze upon the Face of their Majestic Lord.

When the soul is driven up to the collarbone (the agony of death), man truly realizes that it is the moment of parting with the world he has been chasing. There is no longer any physician capable of delaying death even for a second.

Woe after woe to the one who refuses to pray, denies the truth, and walks with arrogance. Was he not once just a drop of emitted semen? The Creator is indeed Most Capable of bringing him back to life from death.

Has man ever realized that there was a time when he was not a thing even mentioned? Allah created him from a despised drop of sperm, endowing him with hearing and sight solely to test him in the world.

Allah has laid out the path of truth very clearly. The decision now rests entirely in man's hands: whether he chooses to submit in gratitude (believe), or turn away in disbelief of his Lord's blessings.

The pinnacle of sincerity of the righteous (Al-Abrar) is when they give the food they love most to the poor, the orphan, and the captive, while their hearts say: 'We do not expect any reward or even a word of thanks from you.'

For their patience in restraining their desires and their earnestness in obedience in the world, Allah rewards them with protection from the horrors of the Day of Judgment, and grants them a Paradise full of peace and garments of silk.
The gradual revelation of the Quran is an education in patience. Be patient in carrying out the decree of your Lord, do not hasten, and do not yield to the whispers of the avid sinners among them.
Allah's oath by the winds sent forth in succession bringing goodness is an affirmation that the promised Day of Recompense will certainly occur; it will not deviate even slightly from His decree.
'Woe on that Day to the deniers.' This phrase, repeated many times, is a severe threat to those who refuse to reflect upon their creation from a despised fluid.
Have We not made the earth a gathering place for the living and the dead? Mountains are firmly set, fresh water is sent down, yet strangely, man continues to deny the Bestower of Blessings.

On that Day, man will no longer be able to speak nor be permitted to offer excuses. That is Yawm al-Fashl (The Day of Decision), where every pretext will be shattered before the absolute justice of Allah.
For the pious, resting under the shades of Paradise and clear springs is the tangible reward for their righteous deeds in the world. 'Eat and drink in satisfaction as a reward for your obedience.'
What are the disbelievers asking one another about? About the 'great news' (the Resurrection) over which they always differ. Soon, they will certainly know its truth with their own eyes.
Mountains as pegs, the night as a covering for rest, and the day for seeking livelihood. Is not the design of this universe a clear proof of His power to resurrect the dead?
