Indeed, the Day of Decision is an appointed time. When the Trumpet is blown, people will come forth in multitudes, the heaven will open, and the mountains will be destroyed, becoming like a mirage.

Hell is lying in wait, becoming a place of return for the transgressors. A place where they will remain for ages without a single drop of cool drink, except boiling water and purulence as an appropriate recompense.

On the Day when the Spirit and the angels stand in rows, not daring to speak except whom the Most Merciful permits. It is a profoundly late regret when the disbeliever wails bitterly: 'Oh, I wish I were dust.'
The angel of death will extract the soul of the disbeliever with a violent and agonizing tear, yet he will draw out the soul of the believer with a gentleness that removes all his worldly burdens.

The Day of Resurrection will certainly arrive. At the first blow of the Trumpet (Ar-Rajifah), the universe will be utterly destroyed, and at the second blow (Ar-Radifah), all creatures will rise with their hearts pounding in terror.

The story of Pharaoh, who arrogantly proclaimed 'I am your most exalted lord,' is an eternal lesson. Allah punished him in this world and the Hereafter as a warning to anyone who feels immune from His power.
Does man not reflect on which is harder to create: their own weak selves, or the heavens that are layered and spread so perfectly without any supporting pillars?

The scale of the Hereafter is abundantly clear: whoever transgresses and prefers the worldly life, Hell will be his abode. But for him who fears the greatness of his Lord and restrains his soul from vain desires, Paradise will be his refuge.

Allah gently reprimanded His most beloved servant (the Messenger of Allah ﷺ) to defend a poor blind man. This is proof that nobility in the sight of Allah is measured by the heart's sincerity to purify the soul, not by social status.
Do not ignore those who sincerely seek the truth merely to curry favor with arrogant rulers or wealthy people who feel no need for Allah's guidance.

Destroyed is man; how intensely he disbelieves! Does he not realize from what he was created? Only from a despised drop of sperm, then Allah made his birth easy. Why does he still arrogantly oppose his Creator?
Observe the food you eat. It is Allah who pours down the rain, cleaves the earth to grow grains, grapes, olives, and dense gardens as an enjoyment for you and your grazing livestock.

When the deafening blast (the Trumpet of Judgment) echoes, every man will flee from his brother, his mother and his father, and his wife and his children. On that Day, every soul will be utterly preoccupied with its own salvation.

The Day of Judgment is marked by the folding up of the sun until its light dims, the falling of the stars, and the destruction of the mountains until they fly like dust. Not a single worldly splendor will remain.

Later on the Day of Recompense, the female infants buried alive due to ignorance will be asked: 'For what sin was she killed?' Allah's justice will not let a single act of oppression escape His reckoning.
When the scrolls of deeds are laid open and the Hellfire is set ablaze, on that Day every soul will know with certainty and see firsthand what it has prepared during its life in the world.

This Quran is not the whisper of a cursed devil, but rather the word of Allah brought by His noble messenger (Angel Jibril), who holds a high rank, is obeyed in the heavens, and is highly trustworthy.

The Quran is a reminder to the worlds, for whoever among you who truly wishes to take a straight path. But know that your will cannot be realized except by the divine success (Tawfiq) and will of Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

When the sky breaks apart, the stars scatter, the seas erupt, and the graves are overturned, then every soul will instantly realize what it has put forward and what it has left behind during its life.

'O mankind, what has deceived you concerning your Most Generous Lord?' This reprimand shakes the heart, making us realize how arrogant man is, even though Allah created him in the most perfect proportion.
Do not think your misdeeds escape observation. Indeed, there are always noble angels (Kiramun Katibin) constantly watching and recording every movement and intention within your heart.

The ultimate destination of mankind is divided into only two streams: the righteous (Al-Abrar) will truly be in a Paradise of bliss, whereas the wicked (Al-Fujjar) will certainly be drowning in the Hellfire.

Do you know how terrifying the Day of Recompense is? It is a Day when no soul will have the power to benefit or defend another in the slightest. All authority and command on that Day belong absolutely to Allah alone.
Woe to those who defraud (Al-Muthaffifin), namely those who, when they take a measure from others, exact full measure, but when they give by measure or weight to others, they cause loss.
Do those defrauders not realize that they will be resurrected on a tremendously dreadful Day? The Day when all mankind will stand with bowed heads before the Lord of the worlds.
The record of deeds of the wicked is truly kept in Sijjin, a precisely written register that will imprison them in the lowest and narrowest depths of the earth.
No! Rather, the stain of the sins and disobedience they constantly earned has covered (rusted) their hearts, so they are blocked from receiving the light of truth.
Conversely, the record of deeds of the righteous (Al-Abrar) is kept high in 'Illiyyin, a noble register witnessed directly by the angels brought near to Allah.
The drink of the inhabitants of Paradise is sealed with fragrant musk. Thus, to attain such eternal bliss, let the competitors race to multiply their obedience while in this world.

When the sky breaks apart and listens obediently to its Lord, and the earth is leveled and casts out all its contents. That is a form of the creation's absolute submission to the will of its Creator on the Day of Judgment.

O mankind, indeed you must labor diligently towards your Lord. For every fatigue in obedience or disobedience in the world, you will certainly meet the recompense for that grueling effort in the Hereafter.
Whoever is given his record of deeds from his right, he will be judged with an easy reckoning, and will return to his family in Paradise with a face beaming with joy.
As for him who is given his record from behind his back because of his evil deeds, he will cry out for destruction, wailing bitterly as he is thrown into the blazing fire of Sa'ir.
Allah's oath by the constellations and the promised Day of Judgment affirms that truth will inevitably triumph, and every injustice will certainly be judged before undeniable witnesses.

The story of the Companions of the Trench (Ashabul Ukhdud) teaches the highest level of steadfast faith. The believers were willing to be burned alive in a trench of fire solely to preserve their belief in Tawhid to Allah, the Exalted in Might.
The threat of Hell is for those who tortured the believers. Yet, look at the generosity of Allah; He still opens the door of repentance if those torturers choose to return before death arrives.
He is Allah, the Oft-Forgiving, the Most Loving (Al-Wadud). His affection and love for His servants who sincerely repent are far greater than His wrath.
This Quran is neither poetry nor magic, but rather an intensely noble word, eternally guarded in its purity from any alteration or deviation within the Preserved Tablet (Lauhul Mahfuzh).
There is not a single soul in this world left without a protector. There are observing angels who constantly record and document every detail of a person's deeds thoroughly.
Let man reflect on the origin of his creation from an emitted fluid. The realization of the frailty of his origin will demolish every shard of arrogance reigning in his chest.
The One capable of creating man from a despised drop of fluid, it is certainly exceedingly easy for Him to revive and resurrect man from bones that have crumbled into the earth.

On the Day of Judgment, all the heart's secrets, hidden intentions, and sins unseen by people in the world will be exposed with glaring clarity. That is the day all that is in the breasts will be made known.
When all secrets are exposed and the reckoning awaits, man will not possess even the slightest power to repel Allah's decree, and there will be no helper capable of defending him.
Exalt the name of your Lord, the Most High. It is He who has created and perfected the form of everything's creation with the most precise and beautiful proportion.
This religion is built upon ease. Allah has promised divine success (Tawfiq) and an easy path for whoever earnestly desires to tread this expansive path of the Sharia.
Deliver the advice and religious warning, for indeed it will tremble the hearts of those who fear Allah, and only the wretched ones will avoid it.

Truly successful and prosperous is the one who constantly purifies his soul from the stains of shirk and disobedience, then remembers the name of his Lord by doing dhikr and establishing prayer with humility.

The greatest disease of mankind is their eyes being deceived by the fleeting splendor of the world. Yet, if they only realized, the life of the Hereafter is far better, more noble, and everlasting forever.

On the Day of Judgment, there will be faces downcast in humiliation and exhaustion. They labored hard in their worldly deeds, but are thrown into Hell because their worship was not based on sincerity and the guidance of the Sunnah.
Yet on the other hand, there will be faces radiant with joy. They are pleased and satisfied seeing the results of their strenuous obedience in the world, now rewarded with a lofty Paradise.
Will man not submit his intellect to reflect? Seeing how the camels are created, the heaven is raised high without pillars, the mountains are set firmly, and the expanse of the earth is spread out?
The primary duty of a servant in da'wah is merely as a warner (mudzakkir). You are by no means a compeller, because the guidance of Tawfiq rests absolutely within the grasp of Allah.
Wherever man runs, indeed, it is only to Allah that their definite return lies. And truly, only before His justice will all deeds be brought to account and reckoned.

No matter how great human civilizations and physical strengths are, if they transgress limits and multiply corruption, surely the whip of Allah's torment will stalk and strike them just like the people of 'Aad and Thamud.

It is a massive error if man assumes that worldly wealth and honor are signs of Allah's love. In reality, the expansion of provision is merely a test to see the extent of his heart's ability to be grateful.

And do not assume that the restriction of provision is a sign of humiliation from Allah. Poverty is a stretch of patience-testing, not a benchmark of a servant's status and honor in the sight of his Lord.
The most heartbreaking scream of regret on the Day of Judgment is the lamentation: 'Oh, I wish I had sent ahead righteous deeds for my life.' A realization that true life only begins after death.

The sweetest call at the end of life is when the angel summons: 'O reassured soul, return to your Lord, well-pleased and pleasing to Him.' That is the grand ticket to enter among His servants and into His Paradise.

Indeed, We have created man into hardship. The nature of worldly life is indeed filled with struggle and fatigue, so make your fatigue count as worship that guides you to the eternal resting place in the Hereafter.
Allah has granted a pair of eyes, a tongue, and a pair of lips, then shown you the two ways (good and evil). The key to salvation is to subdue these blessings to step upon the path of truth.

The steep path that saves man from Hell requires the sacrifice of wealth and the subjugation of the ego. Its form is tangible care, such as freeing a slave or relieving someone from the strangling snare of debt.
The peak of social care intensely loved by Allah is feeding people on a day of severe hunger, especially to an orphan of near relationship or a destitute person desperately marginalized in poverty.
A condition for the perfection of faith is closing the ranks with those who advise one another to patience in obedience, and spread compassion (marhamah) towards fellow creatures on earth.

Allah swears consecutively by the sun, the moon, the day, the night, the heaven, the earth, and the human soul. This majestic oath affirms one truth: that Allah has inspired the human soul with its path of wickedness and its path of piety.
Truly successful is he who purifies his soul. Real success and happiness in the world and the Hereafter can only be attained by subduing desires and adorning oneself with obedience to the Creator.
And truly ruined is he who corrupts his soul. Burying the natural purity of the heart into the mud of disobedience and sin is essentially a shortcut to destruction and eternal regret.

The destruction of the people of Thamud is a historical witness to how terrifying Allah's wrath is. Their arrogance in denying His messenger and slaughtering the miraculous she-camel was recompensed with a torment that leveled them all to the ground.

By the night as it conceals and the day as it brilliantly appears, indeed, the efforts and life paths of mankind are extremely diverse. Some exhaust themselves treading the path to Paradise, while others sprint rapidly towards Hell.
Whoever loves to give of his wealth in the way of Allah, fears Him, and believes in the best reward (Paradise), Allah will surely smooth and ease his path toward happiness.

As for he who stingily withholds his wealth, considers himself self-sufficient (in no need of Allah), and denies the recompense of the Hereafter, Allah will leave him and ease his path toward the abyss of hardship (Hell).
Wealth hoarded with sheer stinginess will absolutely not be able to help or ransom someone when he tumbles and is thrown into the blazing fire of Hell.

Indeed, it is Allah's absolute right to provide guidance to the straight path. And know that to Him alone belongs all the majesty of the Hereafter and all the adornments of this fleeting worldly life.

The pinnacle of a servant's nobility is when he spends his wealth purely to purify his soul, not to return a favor to anyone, but solely seeking the Face of his Lord, the Most High. Soon, he will certainly be well-pleased with his reward.

Your Lord has not taken leave of you, nor has He detested you. When you feel lonely and trials feel suffocating, remember that the delay of help is not a sign that Allah has abandoned His believing servant.
And the Hereafter is better for you than the first [life]. Do not let your worldly sorrow make you forget that all suffering here is only temporary, while the reward with Him is eternal.
And your Lord is going to give you, and you will be satisfied. This promise is the greatest comforter; that your patience today will be paid in full with perfect happiness in Paradise.
Did He not find you an orphan and give [you] refuge? When storms of trials come to cast doubt in your heart, recall the series of blessings and ways out Allah provided in your past.

As a manifestation of gratitude for your Lord's protection and sufficiency: do not oppress the orphan, do not repel the petitioner, and proclaim His blessings as a form of tahadduth bin ni'mah (acknowledging divine favors).

Did We not expand for you your breast? And remove from you your burden? The expansion of the heart to accept the Sharia and destiny is the greatest gift that lifts all burdens of the soul's suffering.

And We raised high for you your repute. Whoever follows the footsteps of obedience of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, Allah will certainly elevate his rank and make his name fragrant among the inhabitants of the heavens and the earth.
For indeed, with hardship [will be] ease. Indeed, with hardship [will be] ease. One hardship will never be able to defeat the two eases that Allah has prepared behind it.
So when you have finished [your duties], then stand up [for worship]. The nature of a believer is not to leave his time empty of beneficial deeds.
And to your Lord direct your longing. Sever your heart's dependence on the praise and pity of creation, and hang all your hopes and reliance purely upon the Owner of the Universe.
We have certainly created man in the best of stature. Allah bestowed intellect, an upright posture, and an eloquent tongue as the primary provisions to recognize and worship his Creator.
However, man can be returned to the lowest of the low (Hell) if he taints his natural purity with shirk and refuses to submit to the Sharia brought by the messengers of Allah.
The only savior from the abyss of humiliation is faith rooted in the chest, proven by righteous deeds. For them, Allah prepares an eternal, uninterrupted reward.
After seeing the clear proof of creation from nothingness into existence, and then resurrection after death, what excuse makes man dare to deny the Day of Recompense?
Is not Allah the most just of judges? It is impossible for Him to leave those who do good without the reward of Paradise, or to let the wrongdoers escape the snare of His Hellfire's justice.
The first revelation (Iqra) is a command to read and learn. However, beneficial knowledge must always be anchored 'in the name of your Lord,' so that intellect does not breed arrogance.
He is Allah who taught by the pen, taught man that which he knew not. The ability to read and write is a magnificent blessing that bridges the transfer of knowledge from one generation to the next.
Know that man will surely transgress when he sees himself self-sufficient and wealthy. Wealth and thrones often anesthetize the heart until it feels it no longer needs the help of its Lord.
Wherever you run chasing the world, indeed, only to your Lord is your return. Before Him, all the piles of wealth that made you arrogant will be brought to account one by one.
Do not obey those who hinder you from obedience. Prostrate and draw near to Allah. For at the lowest point of a servant in his prostration, there he is closest to his Lord.

The Quran was sent down in its entirety from the Preserved Tablet (Lauhul Mahfuzh) to Baitul 'Izzah in the lowest heaven on the Night of Decree (Lailatul Qadr), a night laden with blessings and forgiveness from the Creator.

Do you know how magnificent Lailatul Qadr is? That night is better than a thousand months. The acts of worship, prayers, and charity you perform on that night surpass the value of obedience over 83 years.

On that night, the angels and Angel Jibril descend in multitudes to the earth bringing all matters, blessings, as well as the decrees of provision and lifespan for the coming year by the permission of their Lord.

That night of glory is filled with peace and security from all evil until the emergence of dawn. The angels go around conveying greetings of peace to every believing servant who enlivens the night with obedience.

Those who disbelieved would not leave their misguidance until there came to them clear evidence (Al-Bayyinah), namely the sending of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ who unveiled falsehood with the light of revelation.
That clear evidence is a Messenger from Allah reciting purified scrolls of the Quran, containing upright and just laws, absolute truth, and guidance towards eternal salvation.
Often, division in religion occurs right after the truth has come so glaringly. They divided not out of ignorance, but purely out of envy, ego, and vain desires.

Even though all mankind was commanded only to worship Allah, purifying their obedience (sincerity) to Him in practicing the upright religion, to establish prayer, and to give zakat. That is the foundation of the correct religion.

Indeed, those who disbelieve and reject the truth after the evidence has come, they are the worst of creatures (syarrul bariyya). Their place is to abide eternally in the humiliating torment of Hellfire.

Conversely, those who believe and prove it with righteous deeds are the best of creatures (khairul bariyya). Their most beautiful reward is the Gardens of 'Adn; Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him.

When the earth is shaken with a tremendous earthquake on the Day of Judgment, it will discharge all the heavy burdens from its belly, whether in the form of buried treasures or human bodies interred for centuries.
On that Day, man will stand in bewilderment, asking, 'What is wrong with it?' At that moment, the earth will report all the news about the deeds the children of Adam ever committed upon its back.
The earth testifies by the command of its Lord. It becomes an undeniable witness to every prostration of a believer and every sin of a transgressor, because Allah has inspired it to speak.
Mankind will be resurrected and emerge from their graves scattered, to be shown all the recompense of their deeds directly and clearly without anything being hidden.
So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it. Thus, never belittle a good deed no matter how small, because in the sight of Allah it holds weight on the scale.

Allah swears by the war horses running swiftly, panting and striking sparks of fire from their hooves. This is an allusion to humans who refuse to struggle and sacrifice in the path of their Creator, unlike the beasts that obey their masters.
Indeed mankind, to his Lord, is exceptionally ungrateful (kanud). He easily remembers a single calamity that befalls him, yet forgets the thousands of blessings Allah pours upon him every day.
And indeed, man himself is a witness to his ingratitude through his actions and complaints. The traits of stinginess and worldly greed often radiate from behavior that never feels satisfied.
Man's love for wealth is excessively intense. This blind love frequently blinds his inner sight from the rights of Allah and the rights of others inherent in that wealth.

Does man not realize when all that is in the graves is overthrown and all secrets in the breasts are made apparent? On that Day, indeed, Allah is All-Knowing of their deeds and will recompense them with absolute justice.
The Day of Judgment (Al-Qari'ah) is a heart-striking event that shatters the order of the universe. Do you know how terrifying that Day is when all that was promised truly befalls you?
On that Day, humans resurrected from the graves will be bewildered and scattered like dispersed moths, aimless due to the overwhelming panic that strikes.
The mountains that have firmly pegged the earth will be pulverized until they fly and scatter like tufted wool blown by strong winds. There will be no refuge left in the world.
As for him whose scales of good deeds are heavy, he will be in a pleasant and satisfying life in Paradise. It is righteous deeds based on sincerity that will weigh down those scales.
Conversely, whoever's scales of good deeds are light due to heedlessness in the world, his refuge will be the abyss of Hawiyah, a blazing fire, intensely hot and burning.
The attitude of mutual boasting in accumulating wealth, thrones, and offspring often distracts man from obedience to Allah, until death comes and they enter the graves.

No! You will soon know the dire consequences of your heedlessness. This warning is repeated by Allah so that man may quickly awaken from his long slumber before everything is too late on the Day of Recompense.
If only you knew with 'ilmul yaqin (certain knowledge) about the horrors of the Hereafter, you would never be lulled by the world. Indeed, you will surely see the Hellfire.
Then you will surely see it with 'ainul yaqin (the eye of certainty). There will be no more doubt, and no more room for denial when that torment is displayed directly before the sinners.

On that Day, you will surely be asked about all the pleasures you enjoyed in the world: health, security, free time, food, and drink. Was it for the path of obedience or disobedience that you utilized those blessings?
Allah's oath by time affirms that all mankind is truly in clear loss, because their lifespan continuously decreases yet is often spent on futile things without provision for the Hereafter.
The exception from eternal loss is only for those who fill their time with firm faith and sincerely dedicated righteous deeds, solely seeking the Face of Allah in every breath they take.
Salvation cannot be achieved alone. The third and fourth conditions to be free from loss are advising one another to the truth (religion) and advising one another to patience in facing life's trials.

Imam Shafi'i said, 'If Allah had not sent down any proof to His creation other than this Surah, it would have sufficed them.' This Surah is the most fundamental foundation for the salvation of human life.
Woe to every scorner (humazah) and mocker (lumazah). Those who delight in tearing down the honor of others through gestures, words, or writings are, in essence, inviting destruction upon themselves.
Woe to the one who busies himself accumulating wealth and counting it day and night, as if his wealth will make him immortal in the world and save him from the grip of death.
No! The wealth he boasts of will not help him. He will truly be thrown into Al-Hutamah, the Fire that destroys and crushes everything that enters it.
Do you know what Al-Hutamah is? It is the kindled fire of Allah, which is so intensely hot and terrifying that its pain and burning penetrate right to the deepest core of the human heart.
That Hellfire is closed tight over them. Those stingy mockers and hoarders of wealth will never find a way out, trapped in unceasing torment and endless regret.
They will be tied to extended columns. Such is the humiliating recompense for their arrogance in the world, who felt superior because of piled-up wealth while loving to demean others.

Have you not considered how your Lord dealt with the companions of the elephant? No matter how formidable a plot is aimed at destroying the religion of Allah, it will certainly be foiled and rendered futile by Him.
Allah sent flocks of birds (Ababil) in multitudes to destroy Abrahah's arrogance. A creature no matter how small can become a deadly army of Allah if He so wills.
Those birds pelted them with stones of baked clay (Sijjil). Weapons that seem simple in human eyes became a terrifying torment by the power of the Creator.
Then He made them shattered like eaten straw. This is the tragic end for anyone who challenges the majesty of Allah and attempts to ruin His blessed sacred House.
The story of Ashabul Fiil is a clear proof of Allah's protection over the Kaaba and an eternal warning to tyrannical rulers. No military might can withstand the decree of the Lord of the worlds.
The custom of the Quraysh tribe conducting safe trade journeys in winter to Yemen and summer to Ash-Sham is a immense blessing. Security in commerce is a bounty often forgotten to be appreciated.
So let them worship the Lord of this House. All the blessings of security and economic prosperity should culminate in one thing: submission and pure worship only to the Provider of sustenance.
It is Allah who has fed them, saving them from hunger. The feeling of fullness we experience every day is not solely the result of our sweat, but a direct gift from His generosity.

And He made them safe from fear. The stability of security in a land is the primary condition for the establishment of peaceful worship and commerce. The blessing of safety is an invaluable provision.
If you have been blessed with enough provision to banish hunger and a safe roof to shelter from fear, then fulfill the right of Allah by multiplying gratitude and maintaining obedience to Him.
Have you seen the one who denies the Recompense? Namely, those who are harsh, oppressive, and repulse the orphan without the slightest compassion in their hearts.
The next trait of religious deniers is those who are reluctant, even unwilling, to encourage others to feed the destitute who are starving.
So woe to those who pray, [but] who are heedless of their prayer. They take its time lightly, procrastinate, and perform it without any humility whatsoever.
The hypocrites perform acts of worship purely to be seen (riya); they want to be noticed, praised, and acknowledged by people, not out of sincerity seeking Allah's pleasure.
And they withhold [simple] assistance (al-ma'un). The worst form of stinginess is withholding the loan of trivial items that would not harm them in the slightest if lent to their neighbors.
Indeed, We have granted you (O Muhammad) Al-Kautsar, namely a basin in Paradise and the blessing of immense, overflowing, and uninterrupted goodness.
So pray to your Lord and sacrifice. Make your physical worship (prayer) and your financial worship (sacrifice) pure and sincere only for Allah alone.

Slaughtering sacrificial animals with sincerity is a form of gratitude for the blessing of Al-Kautsar, as well as an act of defiance against the shirk of the polytheists who slaughtered in the name of idols.
Indeed, your enemy is the one cut off (abtar). Whoever hates the Messenger of Allah and his teachings, will in reality be severed from all goodness in the world and in the Hereafter.

Those who oppose the Sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ think that history will forget him. Yet, it is exactly the names of his haters that are disgraced and erased, while the mention of the Prophet ﷺ is continuously elevated on earth and in the heavens.
Say: 'O disbelievers, I do not worship what you worship.' Firmness in Aqidah is the foundation of faith; there is no compromise or bargaining in the matter of purifying Tawhid.
And you are not worshippers of what I worship. Even if polytheists worship Allah, their worship holds absolutely no value because it is mixed with the stain of shirk.
I will never be a worshipper of what you worship. The repetition of this phrase is an eternal declaration that the path of Islam's purity can never be united with the path of falsehood.

For you is your religion, and for me is my religion. Tolerance in Islam is not by blending beliefs or participating in the rituals of other religions, but by leaving them to their choices without coercion.

Surah Al-Kafirun is a surah of disassociation from shirk (Bara'ah minasy syirk). Reciting it before sleep is a sunnah that serves as a sturdy protective fortress for the heart against all forms of disbelief.
When the victory of Allah has come and the conquest. This is a definite promise that Allah's help will always descend to accompany the patience of the sincere and reliant warriors of His religion.

And you see the people entering into the religion of Allah in multitudes. The success of da'wah is not solely the result of the eloquence of human tongues, but rather the guidance exclusively bestowed by Allah.
Then exalt [Him] with praise of your Lord. Every time you achieve success, victory, or complete a good deed, accompany that achievement with dhikr, glorifying His name.

And ask forgiveness of Him (istighfar). Even after just achieving a great victory, a believer is commanded to seek forgiveness so that his heart does not become infected with arrogance and conceit over his own efforts.

Indeed, He is ever Accepting of repentance. The revelation of Surah An-Nasr not only brought glad tidings of victory, but also a subtle sign that the prophetic mission was complete and the passing of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was near.

May the hands of Abu Lahab be ruined, and ruined is he. This story is clear proof that blood relations, no matter how close to a prophet, will not save someone from punishment if they oppose the truth.
His wealth will not avail him or that which he gained. Abundant wealth and high social status instantly lose their value when Allah's curse and wrath have been decreed upon a servant.

Say, 'He is Allah, [who is] One.' This is the pinnacle of the purification of Tawhid. Allah is One in His essence, attributes, and actions; there is absolutely no partner or equal to Him in the entire universe.
Allah, the Eternal Refuge (As-Shamad). All creatures in the heavens and the earth desperately need Him in every beat of their hearts, while He is the Free of need and requires no one.
He neither begets nor is born, nor is there to Him any equivalent. A decisive refutation of all forms of polytheism and ignorance that equate the Creator with His mortal creation.

Say: 'I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak, from the evil of that which He created.' This world is full of perils, and there is no true fortress of protection other than seeking refuge in the Creator.

Seek refuge in Allah from the evil of the darkening night, from the evil of witchcraft, and from the envy of the envier. Envy is a destructive disease of the heart, so fortify yourself with dhikr and du'a.

Say: 'I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, the Sovereign of mankind, the God of mankind.' These three majestic attributes of Allah are the best refuge for every soul feeling weak against the whispers of Satan.
Seek refuge from the evil of the retreating whisperer (Al-Waswasil Khannas). Satan blows doubts when the human heart is heedless, but he will flee and hide when the name of Allah is mentioned.

The whispers of evil into the human chest come not only from the jinn, but also from mankind. Therefore, be selective in choosing close friends, so that you are guarded against instigations that turn you away from His path.
